behavior_summary: Represents the NPC Gorgrond's interactive AI: handles being targeted by spells, reacting to theft, player 'look' descriptions, scheduled idle behavior, fast-area entry/exit triggers, and direct 'use' (talk/interact) flows.
When Gorgrond is U8P_GORGROND::cast upon (Event 11) the script marks the NPC as hostile (sets gorgrondMad) and spawns the spell-combat resolver to handle effects.

Theft by the avatar increments a warning counter (gorgrondWarned) and escalates from verbal warnings to immobilizing the avatar (stasis) and setting Gorgrond's combat target on repeated offenses; directional reactions are spawned toward stolen items.

Look and use interactions produce context-sensitive dialog/Ui behavior: first-meeting flags (gorgrondMet), name/job flags (gorgrondName, gorgrondJob, gorgrondAvaName), and story flags (acolyteKilled) influence available dialog and spawned conversation or response processes.

Schedule and fast-area handlers spawn movement/behavior processes (MOVE/METHOD spawns) unless suppressed by a global scene flag (inDaemonScene). Fast-area enter can immediately set the avatar as target if avatarMasterSorcerer is set.
notable_control_flow: Event-driven: each entry point corresponds to an in-game event (U8P_GORGROND::cast, look, use, schedule, enter/leave fast-area, AvatarStoleSomething) and mostly spawns other processes rather than performing long inline computation.

Escalation flow in AvatarStoleSomething: checks and increments gorgrondWarned, branches across warning->strong warning->stasis/attack outcomes, and spawns movement/attack behaviors when thresholds are reached.

Spawn-based delegation: many actions are implemented by spawning external METHODS (e.g., SPELCMBT::084D, MOVE::0080, METHOD::091C, METHOD::143A) and then suspending, with the spawned process' return used to choose follow-up actions in schedule and dialog flows.
Conditional suppression: global inDaemonScene prevents movement/spawn behavior from schedule and enterFastArea; Npc::isDead checks prevent most actions when Gorgrond is dead.
Dialog branching uses Item::ask result strings and sets persistent story/dialog flags (gorgrondMet, gorgrondName, gorgrondAvaName, gorgrondMad) to influence future interactions.
open_questions: Exact numeric thresholds and textual strings used for each gorgrondWarned level are inferred but not explicitly enumerated (what exact counts map to which escalation step?).

Precise meanings of some spawned METHODs (e.g., METHOD::091C, METHOD::133F, METHOD::143A, METHOD::1090) are inferred (movement, follow-up behaviors, UI handling) but their internal behavior is external to this script.
Whether the U8P_GORGROND::cast parameter is the spell id, caster pid, or an encoded spell-context word is not definitively known (it is passed to SPELCMBT::084D and treated as a uword).
Some internal helper names (e.g., GORGROND::045E referenced by spawn in theft flow) appear in the disassembly but are not fully documented here; their responsibilities are inferred.

main_function.name: U8P_GORGROND::cast
main_function.purpose: Handle being targeted by a spell: mark Gorgrond hostile and hand off detailed spell-combat handling to the spell-combat subsystem.
entry_conditions: Event 11 (U8P_GORGROND::cast) — invoked when a spell is U8P_GORGROND::cast on/targeting Gorgrond; receives a uword parameter (likely spell or caster context).

function_map.name: U8P_GORGROND::cast
function_map.role: Mark Gorgrond hostile and spawn spell-combat resolution (entry for Event 11).

function_map.name: U8P_GORGROND::AvatarStoleSomething
function_map.role: React to avatar stealing an item: free region info, compute direction to stolen item, bark warnings, increment gorgrondWarned, possibly set stasis/target and spawn retaliatory behaviors.

function_map.name: U8P_GORGROND::look
function_map.role: Produce context-aware 'look' descriptions based on flags (gorgrondName, gorgrondJob) and death state.

function_map.name: U8P_GORGROND::schedule
function_map.role: Periodic behavior scheduler: if alive and not inDaemonScene, spawn movement/behavior methods to drive idle/patrol actions.

function_map.name: U8P_GORGROND::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Fast-area entry trigger: set region info, optionally set avatar as target (if avatarMasterSorcerer) or spawn movement reaction (if not suppressed).

function_map.name: U8P_GORGROND::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Fast-area exit cleanup: free/cleanup region info set during enterFastArea.

function_map.name: U8P_GORGROND::use
function_map.role: Handle player 'use' interaction (talk/UI): route to dialog flows, open/close gumps, spawn dialog/behavior processes and set dialog/story flags.

function_map.name: U8P_GORGROND::05A8
function_map.role: Dialog/first-meeting routine: present initial dialog choices, set gorgrondMet/gorgrondName/gorgrondAvaName and adjust gorgrondMad based on responses.

internal_calls.purpose: Spawn follow-up Gorgrond behavior after theft escalation (inferred continuation of retaliatory behavior).
Spawn or route into initial dialog/meeting subroutine when the player uses/talks to Gorgrond.

external_calls.purpose: Spawn the spell-combat/resolution process passing caller pid, incoming spell/caster param, and Gorgrond for effect resolution.
Free previously pushed region/info structure as cleanup at the start of theft handling.
Compute direction from Gorgrond to the stolen item to parameterize a directional reaction/movement spawn.
Spawn a directional movement/animation/attack using the computed direction (reaction to theft).
Immobilize the avatar as a punitive response when theft escalation reaches a certain level.
Make Gorgrond speak warning or threatening lines at the avatar during theft escalation.
Set the avatar as Gorgrond's combat/attention target when escalation requires attack/reactive focus.
Check if Gorgrond is dead to vary the look/description strings accordingly.
Spawn a scheduled behavior process and inspect its result to decide further scheduled actions.
Spawn as a follow-up scheduled behavior depending on METHOD::143A's returned process result.
Immediately set the avatar as target when avatarMasterSorcerer is set (fast-area reaction).
Spawn a fast movement/react behavior when avatar enters the area and movement isn't suppressed by inDaemonScene.
Free/cleanup region info set on enterFastArea (cleanup on leaving the trigger region).
Close an existing interaction UI when use indicates closing the gump (dead/closed states).
Open a dialog/interaction UI with a specific gump id when starting conversation/interaction.
Present dialog choices to the player and branch on the returned string for conversation flow.
Spawn a UI/behavior method as part of the use/dialog flow (exact role inferred: dialog continuation/UI update).
Issue short speech lines (warnings/threats) during theft handling.

reads.name: gorgrondWarned
reads.likely_effect: Read to determine current warning count for theft; controls escalation logic in AvatarStoleSomething.

reads.name: gorgrondMad
reads.likely_effect: Checked to influence dialog/behavior and to determine whether Gorgrond is already hostile (affects spawn choices in use/other handlers).

reads.name: gorgrondName
reads.likely_effect: Controls whether Gorgrond's full name/title or a shorter label is used in look/dialog outputs.

reads.name: gorgrondJob
reads.likely_effect: Controls whether job/title ('Sorcerer'/'mage') appears in look descriptions.

reads.name: avatarMasterSorcerer
reads.likely_effect: If set, causes immediate targeting of the avatar when they enter Gorgrond's fast area.

reads.name: inDaemonScene
reads.likely_effect: Suppresses schedule and fast-area movement/spawn behavior when in special scenes (prevents autonomous actions).

reads.name: acolyteKilled
reads.likely_effect: Alters dialog/interaction branching in use flows (different responses or behaviors if an acolyte is dead).

reads.name: gorgrondMet
reads.likely_effect: Indicates whether the avatar has met Gorgrond before; changes greeting/dialog paths.

writes.name: gorgrondMad
writes.likely_effect: Set (written to value 3 in U8P_GORGROND::cast) to mark Gorgrond as hostile/angry, causing aggressive behavior and altered dialog.

writes.name: gorgrondWarned
writes.likely_effect: Incremented during theft handling to track warnings and drive escalation to stasis/attack.

writes.name: gorgrondMet
writes.likely_effect: Set on first meeting to change future greetings and dialog options.

writes.name: gorgrondAvaName
writes.likely_effect: Set when Gorgrond learns/accepts the avatar's name (affects personalized dialog/look behavior).

writes.name: gorgrondName
writes.likely_effect: Set during conversation to force display of Gorgrond's name/title in future look/dialog outputs.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Represents the NPC Gorgrond's interactive AI: handles being targeted by spells, reacting to theft, player 'look' descriptions, scheduled idle behavior, fast-area entry/exit triggers, and direct 'use' (talk/interact) flows.
